title: Amplify Task Graph
description: The task graph that scripts/task.mjs ingests. Every node carries a required, explicit `type` naming one of the node-type templates declared in schemas/node-types.json (implement, resolve, audit, reduce, agent, fn, expand, switch). The execute-plan dump authors `implement` nodes (an implement-and-audit task); the engine explodes each into <id>.impl and <id>.resolve subnodes, then creates <id>.audit.<i> subnodes at runtime from the audit-resolver's panel. The `resolve`/`audit`/`reduce` node types are DEFINED and validatable here but are created at runtime by the lifecycle. The generalized types (agent, fn, expand, switch) are for the new DAG workflow engine (scheduling and runtime behavior are added in later tasks). Each type's executor is fixed or grammar-validated where applicable; fn/expand/switch have no executor (the engine drives them deterministically). The implement model/tools are NOT stored. On init the engine derives a stable run identifier (runId, printed as GRAPH_ID) from a sha256 of this folded graph plus the --salt text. The engine-internal state fields runId, commitSeq, contentHash, doneHash, generation, dispatchHash, and origin are NOT inputs in this schema.

plan_file.description: Required absolute path to the session plan file. The audit-resolver fetches it (via the resolve-context subcommand) and reads it for this task's design/verification context.

nodes.description: The graph nodes. Each carries a required, explicit `type`; node ids must be unique; every dep must reference an existing node id; the dependency graph must be acyclic.

variables.description: Required dictionary of variable name -> value injected into the audit-resolver's spawn prompt — the capability/approval flags write-plan detects (e.g. $AMPLIFY_CODEX_AVAILABLE), which executor-selection-guidelines.md gates on. Use {} when there are no variables to inject.

node.description: One graph node. Its required, explicit `type` selects which per-type template (implement/resolve/audit/reduce/agent/fn/expand/switch) governs the rest of its properties.

type.description: Required, explicit node type. One of: implement, resolve, audit, reduce, agent, fn, expand, switch. There is no implicit default.

id.description: Unique node id. Must not contain '.', which the engine reserves as the subnode separator (e.g. <id>.impl, <id>.resolve, <id>.audit.<i>).

deps.description: Node ids this node depends on. A successor waits until every dependency is done.

output_schema.description: Minimal typed output declaration. Validated hand-rolled by the engine (no json-schema library). Declares the type of the value a node writes to the value store. When `enum` is present, it defines the exhaustive domain that a downstream `switch` must cover.

enum.description: Optional enumeration of allowed values. When present, the selector is enumerable and a downstream switch must cover every value exactly.

implement.description: An implement-and-audit task authored by the execute-plan dump. Its implementer always runs subagent(general-purpose); specialized GUI/behavioral work is an audit node (authored executor), not an implementer. Auditors are NOT declared here -- they are resolved at runtime by the amplify:audit-resolver agent.

executor.description: Optional. Fixed to subagent(general-purpose) for an implement node; a missing executor defaults to it. Any other value is rejected.

acceptance_criteria.description: Independently checkable statements that define done. Every runtime-resolved auditor verifies each against evidence.

design_aspect.description: Required. The design component this task realizes (the task's `(Aspect: …)` — e.g. Architecture, Data Structure, User Interaction). The audit-resolver develops focus-specific criteria from it.

human_gate.description: Optional. When true, this task requires a human verification gate before it is considered complete.

max_attempts.description: Maximum implement->audit attempts before the task is marked 'failed' (logged, non-halting).

resolve.description: The audit-resolver subnode for a completed task: it designs the auditor panel. Created at runtime, not by the execute-plan dump.

audit.description: One auditor that verifies a task's acceptance criteria against the implementer's diff, blind and read-only. Its executor is an authored sub-agent chosen at runtime by the audit-resolver (any value matching the executor grammar, including the audit-only external-agent drivers).

focus.description: Short focus name for this auditor (e.g. 'technical execution', 'semantic').

audit_prompt.description: The ready-to-run blind-audit prompt the audit-resolver authored for this auditor.

reduce.description: Reduces one audit round to a verdict, keyed by a counter. Its executor names subagent(amplify:audit-reducer) (registry name only; the agent file does not exist yet).

counter.description: The reduce counter (which round this reducer settles).

agent.description: A non-deterministic node dispatched to a subagent. The subagent reads inputs by reference from the value store, executes the prompt, and writes a typed output back. The orchestrator passes only handles in and reads only handles out — output bytes never enter its context.

max_attempts.description: Maximum dispatch attempts before the node is marked failed (non-halting).

fn.description: A deterministic node that runs a pure module export. The engine drives this directly — no subagent is spawned. `module` is the path to the ES module; `export` names the exported function. `require` controls scheduling: 'all-done' (default) waits for every dep to be done; 'all-resolved' (reducer/gather) runs when every dep is resolved (done or failed).

module.description: Path to the ES module containing the function.

export.description: Name of the exported function to call.

require.description: Scheduling policy: 'all-done' (default) waits for every dep; 'all-resolved' runs when every dep is done or failed (reducer/gather).

expand.description: Fan-out combinator: when its upstream node's output exists, the engine reads the `over` node's output (an array), creates one child from `template` per element in a single commit, and wires each child to `gather`. The engine drives this directly — no subagent is spawned.

over.description: Id of the node whose output (an array) drives the fan-out.

template.description: The node template stamped for each element (object); the engine instantiates one child per element.

gather.description: Id of the gather/reduce node that collects the children's results.

switch.description: Branch combinator: when its selector node's output exists, the engine instantiates only the matching case in a single commit. `cases` must exhaustively cover the selector's declared domain (boolean -> {true,false}; enum -> every enum value); validated statically at init. The engine drives this directly — no subagent is spawned.

over.description: Id of the selector node whose output value the switch branches on. That node's output_schema must be enumerable (boolean or enum).

cases.description: Object whose keys are the case values (strings: 'true'/'false' for boolean; enum values for an enum selector). Must exhaustively cover the selector's declared domain with no extras.

executor.description: Names the subagent that runs an audit or agent node. Grammar: subagent(<name>). Auditor executors are chosen at runtime by the amplify:audit-resolver agent; the audit-only external-agent drivers (amplify:codex-driver, amplify:kimi-driver) are valid auditors here, but are barred from the implement slot (the implement type fixes its executor to subagent(general-purpose)). The subagent(...) wrapper reserves room for future executor kinds.
